# Disk Space Golden Vectors

These vectors characterize `PolarDiskSpaceData` conversion from `PbPFtpDiskSpaceResult` and malformed disk-space protobuf payload handling under shared ownership. `platformExpectations` records the current Android Java-protobuf `uint32` signed-int exposure for `fragmentSize` separately from Swift's unsigned `UInt32` behavior so shared code can choose an explicit unsigned policy instead of inheriting either platform accidentally. `DiskSpaceCommonPolicyTest.kt` now executes shared byte-total calculation, zero-fragment behavior, unsigned fragment-size policy, typed malformed-varint parse errors, and `disk-space-readiness.json` behavior-family coverage before production disk-space model code moves.

`disk-space-readiness.json` names the shared-contract behavior-family gate for disk-space model shared ownership: byte-total calculation, free-byte calculation, zero-fragment counts, unsigned 32-bit fragment-size policy, Android signed-fragment and iOS unsigned-fragment platform references, typed malformed-varint parse errors, platform disk-space vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before disk-space model code moves into shared.
